subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the first man created by God according to Genesis?
explanation: Genesis 2:7 records that God formed Adam from the dust of the ground and breathed life into him.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: How many books are in the Protestant Old Testament?
explanation: Protestant Old Testament has 39 books. Catholic has 46 (including deuterocanonical). New Testament has 27.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who led the Israelites out of Egypt?
explanation: Moses led the Exodus from Egypt after the ten plagues. Joshua later led them into Canaan.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Which apostle betrayed Jesus?
explanation: Judas Iscariot betrayed Jesus for 30 pieces of silver (Matthew 26:14-16).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was thrown into the lions' den?
explanation: Daniel was thrown into the lions' den for praying to God against the king's decree. God shut the lions' mouths (Daniel 6).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What is the first book of the New Testament?
explanation: Matthew is the first Gospel and first book of the New Testament.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who built the ark to survive the flood?
explanation: Noah built the ark as instructed by God to save his family and animals from the great flood (Genesis 6-9).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: How many days did God take to create the world?
explanation: God created the world in 6 days and rested on the 7th day (Genesis 1-2).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who killed Goliath?
explanation: David, the shepherd boy, killed the Philistine giant Goliath with a sling and stone (1 Samuel 17).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What is the greatest commandment according to Jesus?
option_a: Love God and love your neighbor
explanation: Jesus said the greatest commandments are to love God with all your heart and love your neighbor as yourself (Matthew 22:36-40).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was swallowed by a great fish?
explanation: Jonah was swallowed by a great fish when he tried to flee from God's command to preach to Nineveh (Jonah 1-2).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: How many apostles did Jesus have?
explanation: Jesus chose 12 apostles (disciples) including Peter, James, John, and Judas Iscariot (replaced by Matthias).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the strongest man in the Bible?
explanation: Samson's strength came from his uncut hair (Nazirite vow). Delilah betrayed him and he lost his strength (Judges 13-16).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Where was Jesus born?
explanation: Jesus was born in Bethlehem, the city of David, fulfilling prophecy (Micah 5:2; Matthew 2:1).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who wrote most of the Psalms?
explanation: King David is traditionally credited with writing 73 of the 150 Psalms.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What is the last book of the Bible?
explanation: Revelation (Apocalypse) is the final book, written by John on the island of Patmos.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was sold into slavery by his brothers?
explanation: Joseph's brothers sold him to Midianite traders, and he ended up in Egypt where he later became vizier (Genesis 37).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What was Peter's original name?
explanation: Peter was originally named Simon. Jesus renamed him Cephas/Peter meaning 'rock' (John 1:42).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the first Christian martyr?
explanation: Stephen was stoned to death for his faith, becoming the first Christian martyr (Acts 7).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: How many days and nights did it rain during the flood?
explanation: Rain fell for 40 days and 40 nights during the great flood (Genesis 7:12).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the mother of Jesus?
explanation: Mary, a virgin betrothed to Joseph, conceived Jesus by the Holy Spirit (Luke 1:26-38).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What was Paul's original name?
explanation: Paul was originally Saul of Tarsus, a persecutor of Christians who converted after encountering Jesus on Damascus road (Acts 9).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the wisest king of Israel?
explanation: Solomon asked God for wisdom to rule Israel, and God granted him unparalleled wisdom (1 Kings 3).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: How many times did Peter deny Jesus?
explanation: Peter denied knowing Jesus three times before the rooster crowed, as Jesus predicted (Matthew 26:69-75).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What are the Beatitudes?
option_a: Blessings taught by Jesus in Sermon on the Mount
explanation: Beatitudes ('Blessed are...') are teachings of Jesus in Matthew 5:3-12 describing qualities of Kingdom citizens.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who wrestled with an angel?
explanation: Jacob wrestled with a divine being at Peniel and received the name Israel, meaning 'struggles with God' (Genesis 32).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What was Jesus' first miracle?
option_a: Turning water into wine at Cana
explanation: Jesus turned water into wine at a wedding in Cana of Galilee (John 2:1-11).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was raised from the dead by Jesus after four days?
explanation: Jesus raised Lazarus from the dead after he had been in the tomb four days (John 11).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What is the fruit of the Spirit?
option_a: Love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, self-control
option_c: Wisdom, understanding, counsel, fortitude, knowledge, piety, fear of Lord
explanation: Galatians 5:22-23 lists the nine fruits of the Spirit produced in believers' lives.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who wrote the Acts of the Apostles?
explanation: Luke, the physician and companion of Paul, wrote both the Gospel of Luke and Acts.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the first king of Israel?
explanation: Saul was anointed by Samuel as the first king of Israel (1 Samuel 10).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: How many sons did Jacob have?
explanation: Jacob had 12 sons who became the heads of the 12 tribes of Israel: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Dan, Naphtali, Gad, Asher, Issachar, Zebulun, Joseph, Benjamin.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the mother of Samuel?
explanation: Hannah prayed fervently for a child and promised to dedicate him to God. She gave birth to Samuel (1 Samuel 1).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What is Pentecost?
option_a: The day the Holy Spirit descended on the disciples
explanation: Pentecost (50 days after Passover) marks the coming of the Holy Spirit and birth of the Church (Acts 2).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the tax collector who climbed a sycamore tree to see Jesus?
explanation: Zacchaeus, a chief tax collector, climbed a sycamore tree and Jesus called him down and visited his house (Luke 19).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who interpreted dreams for Pharaoh?
explanation: Joseph interpreted Pharaoh's dreams of seven fat and lean cows/grains, predicting seven years of plenty followed by famine (Genesis 41).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: How many people were saved on Noah's ark?
explanation: Noah, his wife, his three sons (Shem, Ham, Japheth), and their wives - 8 people total (Genesis 7:13).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the Roman governor who sentenced Jesus to death?
explanation: Pontius Pilate, the Roman prefect of Judea, reluctantly sentenced Jesus to crucifixion (Matthew 27).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What is the shortest verse in the Bible?
explanation: John 11:35 'Jesus wept' is the shortest verse in English Bibles.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the Old Testament prophet who challenged the prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel?
explanation: Elijah challenged 450 prophets of Baal. God sent fire from heaven to consume Elijah's sacrifice (1 Kings 18).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What is the Lord's Prayer also called?
explanation: The Lord's Prayer begins 'Our Father who art in heaven' and was taught by Jesus to his disciples (Matthew 6:9-13).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the disciple known as 'Doubting Thomas'?
explanation: Thomas refused to believe Jesus had risen until he saw and touched Jesus' wounds (John 20:24-29).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who succeeded Moses as leader of Israel?
explanation: Joshua, son of Nun, succeeded Moses and led the Israelites into the Promised Land (Joshua 1).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What is the Armor of God?
explanation: Ephesians 6:10-18 describes spiritual armor: belt of truth, breastplate of righteousness, shoes of peace, shield of faith, helmet of salvation, sword of Spirit.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the woman at the well who met Jesus?
explanation: Jesus met a Samaritan woman at Jacob's well and offered her 'living water' (John 4).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: How many plagues did God send on Egypt?
explanation: Ten plagues: water to blood, frogs, gnats, flies, livestock disease, boils, hail, locusts, darkness, death of firstborn (Exodus 7-12).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who wrote the book of Proverbs primarily?
explanation: Most of Proverbs is attributed to King Solomon, known for his God-given wisdom.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What does 'Emmanuel' mean?
explanation: Emmanuel (Immanuel) means 'God with us,' a prophecy about Jesus in Isaiah 7:14.

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: Who was the first person to see Jesus after resurrection?
explanation: Mary Magdalene was the first to see the risen Christ (Mark 16:9; John 20:11-18).

subject: Christian Religious Studies
question_text: What is the Golden Rule?
option_a: Do to others as you would have them do to you
explanation: Jesus taught: 'So in everything, do to others what you would have them do to you' (Matthew 7:12).
